data.title: Solana's ecosystem, Drift Protocol, suffered an attack, resulting in losses of at least $200 million.
data.description: A security incident occurred at Drift Protocol, a derivatives trading platform based on Solana, with on-chain data showing losses of at least $200 million, and some estimates approaching $270 million. The project team stated that they have detected abnormal activity and are investigating, advising users not to deposit funds into the protocol and emphasizing that "this is not an April Fool's joke." The attack involved multiple liquidity pools, including JLP Delta Neutral, SOL Super Staking, and BTC Super Staking. A single transfer of approximately 41.7 million JLP tokens was worth about $155 million. Additionally, assets such as SOL, USDC, cbBTC, and wBTC were also transferred out. According to statistics, this incident may be one of the largest DeFi attacks in the Solana ecosystem since the Wormhole bridge exploit.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer to Cumberland DRW Observed
data.description: A recent transaction involving Bitcoin has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 02:36, 89 BTC were transferred from an anonymous address, beginning with 168Bve, to Cumberland DRW.

data.title: Morgan Stanley's Spot Bitcoin ETF Awaits SEC Approval for April Launch
data.description: Morgan Stanley has submitted an amended S-1 filing for its branded spot Bitcoin ETF, which is poised for a potential listing on April 8, pending final approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). According to NS3.AI, the ETF is expected to trade under the ticker BITA, as noted by James Seyffart. The approval of this ETF could mark a significant development in the cryptocurrency market, offering investors a new avenue for exposure to Bitcoin.

data.title: Cryptocurrency's Role in National Strategies of North Korea, Russia, and Iran by 2025
data.description: Cryptocurrency is anticipated to play a significant role in the national strategies of North Korea, Russia, and Iran by 2025. According to NS3.AI, Chainalysis has highlighted the increasing importance of digital currencies in these countries' strategic plans. The firm has called for a more proactive approach to identifying and understanding the tools and methods these nations might employ in leveraging cryptocurrency for their national interests.

data.title: Drift Investigates Unusual Activity on Solana-Based DeFi Protocol
data.description: Drift has announced an investigation into unusual activity on its Solana-based decentralized finance (DeFi) protocol. According to NS3.AI, the platform has advised users against depositing funds during this period. Drift emphasized that this warning is not an April Fools joke, underscoring the seriousness of the situation.

data.title: Drift Protocol Experiences Significant Asset Outflow
data.description: More than $270 million in assets were transferred from Drift Protocol's main vault within minutes, reducing the address balance from $309 million to $41 million. According to NS3.AI, Arkham-labeled onchain data revealed that over 15 types of tokens were moved from the Solana-based perpetuals exchange's vault. DefiLlama reported Drift's total value locked (TVL) at approximately $550 million when these transfers were identified. As of the time of writing, Drift had not released a public statement, and security researchers had yet to confirm any exploit.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Warns of Potential Suspension of Ukraine Weapons Support
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a warning regarding the suspension of weapons support to Ukraine. According to NS3.AI, Trump stated that the suspension could occur if European countries fail to join a U.S.-led coalition aimed at securing shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. This warning was reported by the Financial Times.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Invests $17 Billion in US Treasury Bills Amid Cash Reserves Growth
data.description: Berkshire Hathaway has made a significant investment in US Treasury bills, purchasing approximately $17 billion at the latest auction. According to NS3.AI, the firm concluded 2025 with cash and equivalents totaling around $373 billion. Meanwhile, Bitcoin's 20-week rolling correlation with the Nasdaq stood at 0.47 as of Wednesday, indicating a moderate relationship between the cryptocurrency and the stock market.

data.title: Galaxy Digital Reports Minor Security Breach in Development Workspace
data.description: Galaxy Digital has reported a security breach involving unauthorized access to one of its isolated development workspaces. According to NS3.AI, the company confirmed that the financial impact of the breach was minimal, with losses amounting to less than $10,000. Galaxy Digital assured that the compromised environment was not linked to any production systems, trading platforms, or client accounts, thereby minimizing potential risks to its operations and clients.

data.title: EDX Markets Seeks National Trust Bank Charter for Crypto Expansion
data.description: EDX Markets has submitted an application for a national trust bank charter with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency, aiming to enhance its crypto custody and settlement services under federal regulation. According to NS3.AI, the application was made public on April 1. If granted, the charter would enable EDX Markets to offer additional services such as custody, asset management, and principal trading, complementing its current order-matching platform.

data.title: Bank of Canada Faces Dilemma in Balancing CPI Control and Economic Growth
data.description: According to Jin10, the Bank of Canada is grappling with a challenging situation as it attempts to manage consumer price index (CPI) inflation while also supporting economic growth. The recent meeting minutes reveal that officials are encountering conflicts in achieving these dual objectives. The central bank is under pressure to find a balance between curbing inflation and fostering an environment conducive to economic expansion. This situation highlights the complexities faced by policymakers in navigating the current economic landscape.

data.title: Bank of Canada: Economic Growth Faces Downside Risks Amid Oil Price Surge
data.description: The Bank of Canada has released its meeting minutes, highlighting concerns over short-term economic growth facing downside risks. According to Jin10, the committee noted that the recent surge in oil prices poses an additional upward risk to inflation. The central bank's assessment reflects ongoing challenges in balancing economic stability with inflationary pressures.

data.title: Bank of Canada: Rising Gas Prices and Persistent Inflation May Elevate Inflation Expectations
data.description: According to Jin10, the Bank of Canada has released its meeting minutes, highlighting concerns over the potential impact of rising gasoline prices combined with persistently high inflation rates for essential goods. The central bank noted that these factors could lead to increased inflation expectations among consumers and businesses. The minutes reflect ongoing challenges in managing inflationary pressures within the Canadian economy, as policymakers continue to monitor economic indicators closely.

data.title: Naoris Protocol Launches Mainnet with Post-Quantum Cryptography
data.description:
Naoris Protocol has officially launched its mainnet, introducing a layer-1 blockchain that employs post-quantum cryptography for enhanced transaction validation and network security. According to Cointelegraph, the network is currently live with limited, invite-only participation, allowing early users to operate validator nodes and process transactions. This initiative integrates cryptographic standards finalized by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) to mitigate risks associated with existing blockchains, where current encryption methods may become vulnerable over time.

Prior to the mainnet launch, Naoris Protocol's test network processed over 100 million transactions and identified hundreds of millions of potential threats, as reported by the project. This activity spanned millions of wallets and nodes. The system utilizes a consensus model known as distributed proof of security (dPoSec) to verify transactions across nodes, with the NAORIS token supporting network operations as the economic model evolves. The rollout begins with a restricted group of validators and partners, with plans for broader access to expand in phases. The project is advised by experts in cybersecurity, government, and enterprise technology, and is backed by investors including Draper Associates.

The launch coincides with new research suggesting that quantum computing may arrive sooner than anticipated. Revised estimates for quantum computing, which processes information using qubits and quantum states differently from classical computers, are prompting efforts to transition away from current cryptographic standards. A study from Google, released on Monday, indicates that quantum computers may require significantly fewer resources than previously thought to break blockchain encryption. The research found that fewer than 500,000 physical qubits could potentially crack systems securing Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH), a substantial reduction from earlier estimates.

These findings suggest a shorter timeline for quantum risk, with Justin Drake, a researcher at the Ethereum Foundation, estimating at least a 10% chance that a quantum computer could recover a private key by 2032. Researchers at the California Institute of Technology, in collaboration with Oratomic, reached similar conclusions, noting that improvements in error correction could lower the requirements for practical systems to 10,000 to 20,000 qubits, down from earlier assumptions of millions. Based on these reductions, a viable quantum computer could emerge by around 2030. Blockchain developers are beginning to respond, with initiatives such as the introduction of a quantum-resistant vault in the Solana ecosystem and the Ethereum Foundation's launch of a "Post-Quantum Ethereum" resource hub, outlining plans to upgrade the network’s cryptography by 2029.

data.title: CFTC Prepared to Oversee Crypto Industry, Says Chair Michael Selig
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump's nominee for the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), Michael Selig, has expressed the agency's readiness to oversee the $3 trillion cryptocurrency industry. According to Cointelegraph, Selig made this statement on Wednesday, highlighting the CFTC's preparedness to assume responsibility for the crypto market, despite the absence of a timeline for Congress to pass a significant market structure bill.

Selig's remarks come amid the U.S. Senate's deliberations on the CLARITY Act, a crypto market-structure bill currently stalled in committee due to debates over stablecoin yield and other issues. He emphasized that the regulatory clarity being extended to the crypto industry is also being developed for prediction markets, which are regulated by the CFTC under the Commodity Exchange Act. Since his confirmation by the Senate in December, Selig has led the CFTC in adopting policies that suggest a softer approach to the enforcement and regulation of digital assets compared to previous administrations. In March, the CFTC announced a memorandum of understanding with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to coordinate on regulation, including digital assets.

While early drafts of the market structure bill indicated that the CFTC might receive additional authority over digital assets, the SEC is expected to continue regulating cryptocurrencies it deems securities. Meanwhile, U.S. state authorities and federal lawmakers have been scrutinizing prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket for alleged violations of gaming laws and accusations of politicians using insider information for profit. Although many state-level actions are still being litigated, Selig has asserted the CFTC's "exclusive jurisdiction" over prediction markets and has threatened legal action against any challenges to its authority. At a Tuesday event, CFTC enforcement director David Miller stated that the agency views event contracts on prediction markets as "swaps" rather than "gaming," thus falling under its jurisdiction. Some lawmakers have proposed legislation to prevent elected officials with insider information from profiting from event contracts, following suspicious trades related to military actions involving Iran and Venezuela.

data.title: Solana's Price Movement: Key Supply Clusters Absorbed
data.description: Solana (SOL) traded slightly above $84 on April 1. According to NS3.AI, Glassnode data indicated that approximately 37.7 million SOL were acquired between $85.31 and $88.22. A sustained close above $89.82 would signify that the three supply clusters have been absorbed.

data.title: Bitcoin Surpasses 69,000 USDT with Daily Increase
data.description: Bitcoin has exceeded the 69,000 USDT mark, currently trading at 69,025.76 USDT. According to Foresight News, this represents a daily increase of 1.11%.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Spot Silver Rises Over 1% to $75.92 per Ounce
data.description: Spot silver prices have increased by more than 1% today, currently trading at $75.92 per ounce. According to Jin10, this rise in silver prices reflects ongoing market dynamics and investor sentiment. The precious metal's performance is being closely monitored by traders and analysts as they assess its potential impact on broader market trends.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 69,000 USDT with a 1.71% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 01, 2026, 17:03 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 69,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 69,002.648438 USDT, with a narrowed 1.71% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Initiates Implementation of Stablecoin Innovation Act
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury has officially commenced the implementation of the Stablecoin Innovation Act, known as the GENIUS Act, on April 2. According to BlockBeats, the Treasury released a proposed rulemaking notice, marking the first regulation under the act, which spans 87 pages and opens a 60-day public comment period.

Under the GENIUS Act, issuers of payment stablecoins with a total issuance not exceeding $10 billion can opt for state-level regulatory systems, provided that the state's regulatory framework is 'substantially similar' to the federal framework. The U.S. Treasury aims to establish broad principles for determining 'substantial similarity' through this notice.
